name: Hanging Obelisk of Ramses II
description: A monumental red granite obelisk of Ramses II displayed in a unique suspended position, symbolizing royal authority and divine power.

name: Colossus of Ramses II
description: A colossal statue weighing over 80 tons, representing Ramses II in a majestic standing pose at the museum entrance.

name: Victory Column of Merneptah
description: A historical monument celebrating the military victories of King Merneptah, inscribed with significant achievements.

name: Statue of Senusret I
description: A refined statue representing Senusret I, reflecting the artistic precision of the Middle Kingdom.

name: Head of Amenhotep III
description: A sculpted royal head of Amenhotep III showing idealized features and fine craftsmanship.

name: Statue of Hatshepsut
description: A powerful depiction of Queen Hatshepsut in kingly form, emphasizing her authority as a female pharaoh.

name: Statue of Thutmose III
description: A statue of Thutmose III, one of Egypt’s greatest military rulers, symbolizing strength and expansion.

name: Seated Statue of Ramses II
description: A seated statue of Ramses II representing stability, divine rule, and timeless authority.

name: Statue of Seti II
description: A statue of Seti II reflecting the artistic traditions and royal continuity of the late New Kingdom.

name: Statue of Ptolemy II
description: A statue of Ptolemy II combining Egyptian artistic tradition with Hellenistic influence.

name: Statue of Arsinoe II
description: A royal statue of Queen Arsinoe II reflecting the fusion of Egyptian and Hellenistic artistic styles.

name: Double Statue of Ramses II and Ra-Horakhty
description: A symbolic double statue showing Ramses II alongside the sun god Ra-Horakhty, emphasizing divine kingship.

name: Statue of Amenemhat III
description: A powerful statue of Amenemhat III highlighting the strength and realism of Middle Kingdom sculpture.

name: Statue of Nectanebo I
description: A statue of Nectanebo I representing the last native Egyptian dynasties before foreign rule.

name: Statue of Merneptah
description: A statue of King Merneptah emphasizing royal authority and continuity after Ramses II.

name: Statue of Emperor Augustus
description: A Roman-era statue of Emperor Augustus depicted in Egyptian royal style, showing cultural integration.

name: Statue of Senusret III
description: A striking statue of Senusret III known for its realistic facial features and powerful expression.

name: Royal Granite Statue (Unidentified King)
description: A finely carved granite statue representing an unidentified king, showcasing idealized royal features.

name: Column of Sahure
description: A column from the temple of King Sahure, decorated with intricate relief carvings.

name: Hathor Column Capital from Dendera
description: A beautifully carved column capital depicting the face of the goddess Hathor, symbolizing joy and music.

name: Papyrus Column from Old Kingdom
description: A papyrus-shaped column symbolizing the marshlands of creation in ancient Egyptian cosmology.

name: Gateway of Amenemhat III
description: A monumental gateway belonging to Amenemhat III, reflecting the grandeur of Middle Kingdom architecture.

name: Offering Relief of Ramses II
description: A carved relief showing Ramses II presenting offerings to the gods, symbolizing devotion and divine favor.

name: Mini Limestone Pyramidion
description: A small pyramidion that once crowned a pyramid, symbolizing the sun’s rays and rebirth.

name: Statue of Hapi (Nile God)
description: A statue of Hapi, the Nile god, shown carrying offerings symbolizing fertility and abundance.

name: Lintel of Ramses III
description: A granite lintel from the reign of Ramses III decorated with inscriptions and royal titles.

name: Statue of Sekhmet
description: A powerful statue of the lion-headed goddess Sekhmet, symbolizing war and healing.

name: Statue of Ptah
description: A statue of Ptah, the creator god, depicted in mummiform shape holding a staff of power.

name: Statue of Min
description: A statue of the fertility god Min, associated with regeneration and agricultural prosperity.

name: Column from Medinet Habu
description: A decorated column from Medinet Habu, featuring reliefs of Ramses III and religious scenes.

name: Head of Goddess Hathor
description: A finely carved head of the goddess Hathor, symbolizing love, music, and motherhood.

name: Statue of Anubis
description: A statue of Anubis, the jackal-headed god of mummification and the afterlife.

name: Small Obelisk of Ramses II
description: A smaller obelisk dedicated to Ramses II, inscribed with royal cartouches and solar symbols.

name: Triad Statue of Ramses II with Two Deities
description: A triad statue showing Ramses II flanked by two gods, symbolizing divine protection and kingship.

name: Ramses II Offering Statue
description: A statue of Ramses II presenting offerings, demonstrating his devotion to the gods.

name: Thutmose III with White Crown
description: A statue of Thutmose III wearing the White Crown of Upper Egypt, symbolizing rule over Upper Egypt.

name: Amenhotep II before Weret Hekau
description: A statue showing Amenhotep II standing before the goddess Weret Hekau, emphasizing divine legitimacy.

name: Horemheb before Amun
description: A statue of King Horemheb standing before the god Amun, reinforcing his divine support.

name: Seti I with Isis
description: A statue of Seti I accompanied by the goddess Isis, symbolizing protection and divine blessing.

name: Ramses II as a Child with Hauron
description: A statue showing Ramses II depicted as a child under the protection of the god Hauron.

name: Osiris Enthroned Statue
description: A seated statue of Osiris, god of the afterlife, symbolizing resurrection and eternal life.

name: Horus Falcon Statue
description: A powerful representation of Horus in falcon form, protector of the king and symbol of kingship.

name: Isis Nursing Horus
description: A tender depiction of Isis nursing the infant Horus, symbolizing motherhood and divine protection.

name: Outer Sarcophagus of Seti I
description: A massive outer sarcophagus carved from stone, designed to protect the body of Seti I for eternity.

name: Inner Coffin of Tutankhamun
description: The innermost golden coffin of Tutankhamun, richly decorated with protective deities and spells.

name: Anthropoid Coffin of Psusennes I
description: A human-shaped coffin made of silver, belonging to King Psusennes I, one of the most precious royal burials.

name: Wooden Coffin of a Priest
description: A painted wooden coffin belonging to a priest, covered with funerary texts and divine imagery.

name: Stone Sarcophagus Lid
description: A heavy stone lid carved with the image of the deceased and protective inscriptions.

name: Nested Coffins Set
description: A set of nested coffins placed one inside another, designed for maximum protection in the afterlife.

name: Canopic Chest
description: A chest used to store the internal organs of the deceased, guarded by the Four Sons of Horus.

name: Quartzite Sarcophagus of Thutmose I
description: A quartzite sarcophagus belonging to Thutmose I, richly decorated with protective spells.

name: Coffin of Tiye
description: Wooden coffin of Queen Tiye, adorned with gilded funerary inscriptions and divine motifs.

name: Coffin of Priest Badiuaser
description: Coffin of the priest Badiuaser, covered with hieroglyphic texts and protective divine images.

name: Granite Sarcophagus from Dynasty 26
description: A massive granite sarcophagus from the 26th Dynasty, designed to protect the deceased's body for eternity.

name: Coffin of Hatshepsut (before modification)
description: The original coffin of Queen Hatshepsut before alterations, showcasing exquisite New Kingdom artistry.

name: Granite Coffin of Nectanebo II
description: A solid granite coffin from the last native pharaoh of Egypt, Nectanebo II, reflecting Late Period craftsmanship.

name: Painted Wooden Coffin Lid from Deir el-Bahari
description: A painted lid of a wooden coffin, decorated with colorful motifs and hieroglyphic texts for protection.

name: Sarcophagus of Psamtik
description: A limestone sarcophagus belonging to Psamtik, adorned with hieroglyphs and divine imagery.

name: Anthropoid Sarcophagus with Book of the Dead Texts
description: An anthropoid sarcophagus featuring hieroglyphs from the Book of the Dead to guide the deceased in the afterlife.

name: Sarcophagus of Ramesses III (partial)
description: Part of the sarcophagus of Ramesses III, illustrating funerary texts and divine protection symbols.

name: Tutankhamun's Inner Golden Coffin
description: The innermost coffin of Tutankhamun, made of solid gold, adorned with protective deities and inscriptions from the Book of the Dead.

name: Tutankhamun's Middle Coffin (Gilded Wood)
description: A gilded wooden coffin that encases the inner coffin, decorated with gods and hieroglyphic spells.

name: Tutankhamun's Outer Quartzite Coffin
description: The outer quartzite coffin that protected the king's mummified body, adorned with funerary symbols.

name: Tutankhamun's Golden Throne
description: The boy king's throne, covered with gold leaf and scenes depicting Tutankhamun receiving gifts from the gods.

name: Tutankhamun's Chariot
description: A ceremonial chariot belonging to Tutankhamun, richly decorated and used in royal processions and hunts.

name: Tutankhamun's Ritual Chariot
description: A smaller chariot used in ceremonial rituals, highlighting the boy king's divine role.

name: Tutankhamun's Ka Statue
description: A black statue representing Tutankhamun's Ka (spirit), symbolizing protection and divine presence.

name: Tutankhamun's Canopic Alabaster Jars
description: Alabaster jars used to store the king's internal organs, protected by the Four Sons of Horus.

name: Tutankhamun's Funerary Beds
description: Three elaborately carved beds featuring animal heads, including lions, cows, and hippos, representing divine guardianship.

name: Tutankhamun's Fan with Golden Handle
description: A ceremonial fan made from ostrich feathers with a gold handle, symbolizing royalty and ceremonial grandeur.

name: Tutankhamun's Meteorite Dagger
description: A dagger crafted from meteoric iron, symbolizing the king's divine protection and celestial connection.

name: Tutankhamun's Winged Scarab Necklace
description: A golden necklace featuring a winged scarab, symbolizing rebirth and protection in the afterlife.

name: Tutankhamun's Seated Anubis Statue
description: A statue of the god Anubis seated on a shrine, guarding the king's remains and symbolizing mummification rituals.

name: Tutankhamun Hunting Scene Model
description: A miniature model depicting Tutankhamun hunting with a spear, showcasing royal leisure and power.

name: Tutankhamun Funerary Boat Models
description: Miniature replicas of funerary boats, used for transporting the king's spirit in the afterlife.

name: Tutankhamun Hunting Shield
description: A ceremonial shield used during royal hunting, decorated with gold and hunting scenes.

name: Tutankhamun Bow and Arrows
description: The king's ceremonial bow and set of arrows, symbolizing royal power and martial skill.

name: Tutankhamun's Golden Sandals
description: A pair of gilded sandals, worn by the king, reflecting luxury and ceremonial significance.

name: Tutankhamun's Royal Gloves
description: A pair of ceremonial gloves symbolizing the king's authority and divine protection.

name: Tutankhamun Senet Board
description: A Senet game board made for the king, reflecting the belief that this game guided the soul in the afterlife.
material: Wood with ivory and gold inlay

name: Tutankhamun Silver Trumpet
description: A ceremonial silver trumpet used in royal rituals and ceremonies, highlighting the king's divine authority.

name: Tutankhamun Bronze Trumpet
description: A bronze ceremonial trumpet, used alongside the silver trumpet during rituals, symbolizing royal majesty.

name: Tutankhamun Childhood Chair
description: A small chair used by Tutankhamun as a child, elaborately decorated with gold and symbolic motifs.

name: Tutankhamun Blue Glass Headrest
description: A blue glass headrest used to support the king's head in his tomb, symbolizing protection in the afterlife.

name: Tutankhamun Ushabti Figurine
description: A small wooden Ushabti statue representing the king, intended to serve him in the afterlife.

name: Tutankhamun Gold Collar
description: A broad gold collar worn by the king, inlaid with colored stones, representing divine protection and prestige.

name: Tutankhamun Alabaster Perfume Vessel
description: A delicately carved alabaster vessel used to store perfumes for the king, symbolizing luxury and ritual use.

name: Tutankhamun Half-Body Wooden Bust
description: A half-body wooden bust of Tutankhamun, representing the young king in ceremonial attire.

name: Tutankhamun Scarab Ring
description: A gold ring featuring a scarab beetle, symbolizing rebirth and protection for the king in the afterlife.

name: Tutankhamun Game Box
description: A small gilded box containing game pieces, associated with the ancient Senet game played by the king.

name: Tutankhamun Nemes Headdress
description: The famous golden 'Nemes' striped headcloth worn by Tutankhamun, symbolizing kingship and divine protection.

name: Tutankhamun Falcon Horus Statue
description: A gilded wooden statue of the falcon god Horus, representing protection and divine guidance for the king.

name: Tutankhamun Ivory Box
description: A delicately carved ivory box, likely used for cosmetic or ritual purposes in the king's tomb.

name: Tutankhamun Gilded Game Pieces
description: Miniature gilded game pieces from the Senet game, symbolizing the journey of the soul through the afterlife.

name: Tutankhamun Pottery and Wine Vessels
description: A set of pottery vessels that once contained wine and oils, showcasing daily and ritual life of the king.

name: Khufu's Solar Boat
description: The oldest intact wooden vessel in the world, used in the funerary ritual of Pharaoh Khufu to sail into the afterlife.

name: Statue of Young Khufu
description: The only known statue of Pharaoh Khufu, representing him as a young ruler.

name: Queen Hetepheres Collection
description: A collection of items including her bed, chair, and box, illustrating royal domestic life.

name: Scribe Statue (GEM Copy)
description: A detailed statue of an Egyptian scribe, reflecting administrative life in ancient Egypt.

name: Yuya and Thuya Statues
description: Statues of the grandparents of Pharaoh Akhenaten, important figures in the royal family.

name: Papyrus of Hunefer (Book of the Dead)
description: A papyrus scroll detailing funerary spells and illustrations from the Book of the Dead.

name: Victory Stele of Merenptah
description: A monumental stele celebrating Pharaoh Merenptah's military victories and divine favor.

name: Statues of the 25th Dynasty Kushite Kings
description: A collection of statues representing kings of the 25th Dynasty, showing Nubian influence.

name: Jewelry of Lahun Princesses
description: A set of exquisite jewelry pieces found in the tombs of princesses at Lahun, illustrating craftsmanship.

name: Statue of Amenemhat III (Lion Head)
description: A unique statue of Pharaoh Amenemhat III with a lion-headed representation, emphasizing royal strength.

name: Fayum Portraits
description: A selection of Roman-period mummy portraits from Fayum, illustrating personalized burial art.

name: Amarna Statues (Akhenaten & Nefertiti)
description: A group of statues from the Amarna period, showcasing Pharaoh Akhenaten and Queen Nefertiti.

name: Tutankhamun's Ceremonial Shield
description: A gilded ceremonial shield decorated with a scene of the king as a sphinx trampling enemies, symbolizing royal protection.

name: Tutankhamun's War Chariot
description: A lightweight military chariot used for high-speed maneuvers, showcasing the advanced technology of the New Kingdom.

name: Gilded Statue of God Ptah
description: A divine statue of Ptah found in Tutankhamun's tomb, covered in gold leaf and representing the creator god.

name: Tutankhamun's Ceremonial Gloves
description: Exquisite linen gloves found in the king's treasury, showcasing the intricate textile work of the 18th Dynasty.

name: Gold Mask of Psusennes I
description: A magnificent funerary mask of solid gold and lapis lazuli, found in the royal necropolis of Tanis.

name: Silver Coffin of Sheshonq II
description: A rare falcon-headed silver coffin belonging to King Sheshonq II, highlighting the 'Tanis Gold' collection.

name: Statue of King Djoser
description: The oldest life-size royal statue, depicting King Djoser seated in a heb-sed robe, originally from his Step Pyramid.

name: Menkaure Triad
description: A high-relief statue showing King Menkaure between the goddess Hathor and a personified district (Nome) of Egypt.

name: Statue of Ka-Aper (Sheikh el-Balad)
description: A masterpiece of wood carving depicting the priest Ka-Aper with remarkably realistic crystal-inlaid eyes.

name: Statue of Dwarf Seneb and his Family
description: A famous sculpture representing the dwarf official Seneb, his wife, and children, illustrating social inclusion in ancient Egypt.

name: Colossal Statue of Amenhotep III and Tiye
description: One of the largest family statues in history, depicting the king and queen seated with their daughters at their feet.

name: The Satirical Papyrus
description: A unique papyrus scroll using animal fables as social satire, showing a world where animals mimic human hierarchy.

name: The Rosetta Stone (Interactive Digital Model)
description: The key to deciphering ancient Egyptian hieroglyphs, featuring three scripts: Hieroglyphic, Demotic, and Greek.
